item.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.

item.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

item.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Stanford’s latest AI Index shows a widening gap between experts and the public, with rising anxiety over jobs, healthcare, and the economy.

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch signals IPO readiness as AI agents fuel revenue surge
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: While many startups founded prior to the emergence of ChatGPT are struggling to position themselves for the AI era, Vercel, a 10-year-old dev tool and website hosting platform, is benefiting from the explosion of AI-generated apps and agents.

item.title: The largest orbital compute cluster is open for business
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Kepler Communications is flying 40 GPUs in Earth orbit. And its latest customer is Sophia Space.

item.title: Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic’s Mythos model
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The report is particularly surprising since the Department of Defense recently declared Anthropic a supply-chain risk.

item.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

item.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
item.dc:creator: Natasha Lomas, Romain Dillet, Kyle Wiggers, Lucas Ropek
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: At the HumanX conference, everyone was talking about Claude
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Anthropic was the star of the show at San Francisco's AI-centric conference.

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

item.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
item.dc:creator: Cindy Zackney
item.description: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. Expect live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on autonomous driving's software revolution, deep dives into cyber defense and climate tech, and candid conversations about how AI is rewriting the global music and anime industries.

item.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: ChatGPT had reportedly been used to plan the attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University last April. The family of one victim has said that they plan to sue OpenAI over the incident.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now it's No. 5 — and rising.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world. Are real cybersecurity concerns a cover for a bigger problem at the frontier lab?

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.
